results.possibleIdentity: Hayden Ross Carter (1944–1990) was an Australian artist, poet, and editor who was a significant figure in the experimental art and literary scenes of the late 1960s and 1970s. He is most noted for his efforts to bridge the gap between art and technology, specifically as a founding member and the prime mover behind the Adelaide chapter of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which was established in 1968 as the first branch of the organization in Australia. Carter authored the primary report on the group's activities, titled "The Adelaide E.A.T. Group: First Year 1968." Later based in Melbourne, he founded and edited the influential arts and literary magazine "Contempa" (active approximately 1972–1978), which published avant-garde poetry and visual art. His own artistic practice included printmaking, concrete poetry, and participation in experimental exhibitions such as the Mildura Sculpture Triennial during its periods of technological focus.
